task: Implement a JavaScript function `isValidSemver(s)` that returns the boolean `true` if and only if `s` is a strictly valid Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 version string per semver.org §2 and the §9 backus-naur grammar, and `false` otherwise. Rules, all required: (1) The core is `major.minor.patch`, three dot-separated numeric identifiers; each is a non-negative integer with NO leading zeros — `0` is valid but `00`, `01`, `1.02.3`, `01.2.3` are INVALID. (2) An OPTIONAL pre-release follows a single `-`: a dot-separated series of identifiers, each NON-EMPTY and made only of `[0-9A-Za-z-]`; an identifier that is purely numeric must NOT have a leading zero (`01` invalid as a pre-release id, but alphanumeric `0a` is fine). Empty identifiers (a trailing `-`, a leading `.`, or `..`) are INVALID. (3) An OPTIONAL build metadata follows a single `+`: a dot-separated series of identifiers, each NON-EMPTY and made only of `[0-9A-Za-z-]`; leading zeros ARE allowed in build identifiers. Empty build identifiers are INVALID. (4) No other characters anywhere; no leading or trailing whitespace; non-ASCII letters are invalid. Return `false` for anything that breaks any rule. These are adversarial cases that a lax-but-plausible validator wrongly accepts: `1.2` (missing patch), `1.2.3.4` (extra field), `01.2.3` / `1.02.3` / `1.2.03` (leading-zero core), `1.2.3-` (empty pre-release), `1.2.3-01` (numeric pre-release id with leading zero), `1.2.3-alpha..1` / `1.2.3-alpha.` / `1.2.3-.alpha` (empty identifier), `1.2.3+` / `1.2.3+build..1` (empty build identifier), `v1.2.3` (leading v), `1.2.3 ` (trailing space), `1.2.-3` (negative), `1.2.3-α` (non-ASCII), and the empty string — all must return `false`, while `1.2.3`, `0.0.0`, `1.2.3-alpha.1`, `1.2.3-0.3.7`, `1.2.3+build.1`, and `1.2.3-beta+exp.sha.5114f85` return `true`. Return ONLY the function source as text. Do NOT create or write any files.
visibleSpec: isValidSemver(s) -> boolean. true iff s is a strict SemVer 2.0.0 string: major.minor.patch each a non-negative integer with no leading zeros ('0' ok, '01' invalid); optional '-prerelease' of non-empty dot-separated [0-9A-Za-z-] identifiers where a purely-numeric id has no leading zero; optional '+build' of non-empty dot-separated [0-9A-Za-z-] identifiers (leading zeros allowed). Empty identifiers, extra/missing core fields, leading-zero numerics, non-ASCII, surrounding whitespace, or empty string all return false.
scope: Single pure function returning a boolean. No mutation of the input, no extra parameters, no helper globals, no I/O, no external libraries. Every malformed input returns false rather than throwing.
